title: Internal: Always use copied AstClass node for parameterization
body:
Hi,
In V3Param, when the param processor encounters reference to parameterizable classes without parameter overrides, it copies the original AstClass node and use the uncopied one as the class instance, since the constant propagation may modify the referenced node. And as [this comment](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/pull/4179#issuecomment-1539672463) said, rather than returns the copied one to the referencing node and make future copies with the original node, it returns the original one because there're some links to it that can not be traversed. But the problem probably has been fixed by #4170, #4440 and other recent patches, and I haven't observed anything wrong about it now. So perhaps we can use the other method, for the benefits that it's easier to handle node relink (it's not needed to use one more map to find the original node), and don't need to use AstClass::user4p to store the copied node. Since these two methods always need to make a copy for class references without parameter overrides, there shouldn't be any performance drawback for the new one.
